_meta.name: hexa-lang 인터프리터 의미론 검증 — eval_clm 세션
_meta.description: P5c-1 서빙 엔드포인트 (eval_clm.hexa) 구현 중 발견한 hexa 인터프리터 의미론 7건과 P5c-4 레이턴시 측정 데이터 포인트. 전부 /tmp에서 최소 재현 확인 완료.
_meta.session: 2026-04-11 train_and_save.hexa → train_byte_clm.hexa → eval_clm.hexa → measure_latency.hexa 파이프라인 완성
_meta.rule: 이 findings는 향후 hexa 학습/추론/서빙 .hexa 작성 시 필수 참고

findings.title: hexa lists are pass-by-value
findings.symptom: Helper function `fn train_one_pair(W, token, target)` mutating `W[row_start + j] = ...` had no effect on caller's W — loss stayed exactly at log(vocab) across 200 steps.
findings.root_cause: List values are copied when passed to a function. Index-mutation inside the callee writes to the local copy. The caller's binding is never touched.

workarounds.approach: inline into main's scope where `let mut` lives
workarounds.evidence: training/train_and_save.hexa line 200-250 inlines SGD update → loss drops 2.08 → 0.008 in 82 ms

workarounds.approach: return the updated list, caller reassigns
module-level `let mut` + helper assigns from inside
workarounds.evidence: eval_clm.hexa install_tensor(name, data) writes module-level `embed`/`L0_Wq`/... /tmp/modmut_test.hexa confirms module-level vars can be reassigned AND index-mutated from inside functions
training/train_gpu_pure.hexa uses alloc_raw + write_f32 for all weights

findings.verification: After inlining SGD into main() in train_and_save.hexa: loss 2.079 → 0.008 in 30 steps (82 ms), top1 11% → 100%, PASS

findings.title: args() indexing: [0]=binary, [1]=script, [2]=first user arg
findings.symptom: `hexa serving/eval_serve.hexa -- /tmp/foo.ckpt` loaded the DEFAULT model, not the checkpoint. Looked like a checkpoint loader bug.
findings.root_cause: hexa args() returns the FULL argv including the hexa binary path at [0] and the script at [1]. No `--` separator convention. First user argument is always at index 2.
findings.rule: Read CLI args with `if len(_argv) >= 3 { path = _argv[2] }`. Never pass `--` as an argument separator.

findings.title: hexa interpreter uses process cwd for relative paths, not script dir
findings.symptom: Background training command `$HEXA training/train_byte_clm.hexa` exited with 'Error reading training/train_byte_clm.hexa: No such file or directory' even though the file existed at $ANIMA/training/train_byte_clm.hexa. Cause: outer shell cwd was $HEXA_LANG.
findings.root_cause: hexa resolves relative script paths against the caller's getcwd(), not against anything script-relative. Same applies to read_file/write_file inside the script.
findings.rule: Always use absolute paths in Bash tool calls when invoking hexa from multiple directories. For cross-repo work (anima + hexa-lang) this is the default failure mode.

findings.title: String concatenation is O(1) amortized — safe for 100 KB+ accumulation
findings.symptom_before: Worried that building 80 KB checkpoint via `s = s + token_str` in a loop would be O(n²) and make save_checkpoint the dominant cost.

benchmark.complexity: linear in total output size — rope or amortized doubling

findings.implication: train_byte_clm.hexa save_checkpoint writes 100 KB HEXACKPT-v1 in <100 ms. No need for write_file_bytes or chunked output.

findings.title: Module-level `let mut` can be reassigned AND index-mutated from inside functions
findings.implication: eval_clm.hexa's install_tensor(name, data) pattern (module-level `let mut embed = tensor_zeros(...)` + `if name == 'embed' { embed = data }`) is correct hexa semantics. Same pattern used by eval_serve.hexa's W_DATA binding.

findings.title: Multi-statement if/while blocks with `;` separator work
findings.implication: while loops can compress inner body: `while i < n { out[i] = f(i); i = i + 1 }`

findings.title: CPU interpreter per-token latency baseline

measurement.model: 2-layer transformer d=16 ff=32 vocab=32 seq=8 rank=4 (19 tensors, 100 KB ckpt)
measurement.verdict: FAIL — 6.85x slower than P5c-4 200 ms/token target

native_estimate.rationale: P5b hexa-lang interpreter→native speedup demonstrated 500x for comparable ops (alphabeta3 + BLIS, DD175)

findings.implication: P5c-4 target is unreachable on the mac interpreter. Native build (hexa-build + BLIS) would put us at ~2.74 ms/token — 73x UNDER the target. H100 GPU path would be even faster. Critical path: bench on native first, then on H100.
